goal: Hub i18n: render EN at root + ZH at /zh/, language toggle in header, intent content stays as-written

summary.title: Hub i18n: EN at root + ZH at /zh/ with header toggle, intent content preserved
summary.what: Add per-language render pass to Hub. internal/hub/i18n.go centralises a ~50-key translation map; pageCtx gains Lang + OtherLangPath + OtherLangLabel; renderAll iterates SupportedLanguages and writes EN to dir/ and ZH to dir/zh/; rootPathFor + otherLangPath compute the (lang × depth) directory math so nested pages reach /assets and the toggle round-trips both ways. Header gains a lang-toggle button; base.html / index.html chrome use {{t .Lang key}} for translated strings.
summary.why: User asked for Hub to support both languages. Following the existing agent language rule (intent content stays as-written; only UI chrome translates) keeps the corpus identical across renders — readers in either language see the same intent records, just different navigation.

decisions.chose: EN at root, ZH under /zh/, assets and data shared at root
decisions.rationale: EN-at-root preserves all existing links / bookmarks / docs; /zh/ for the second language is a standard static-site convention; assets and data have no UI text so duplicating them is pointless

decisions.chose: centralise in i18n.go, key by dot-namespaced strings, render-time lookup
decisions.rationale: small fixed string set (~50 keys); a real i18n library would be over-engineered. Future extraction is one file walk.

decisions.chose: do NOT translate user-authored title / what / why / decisions / risks / anti_patterns
decisions.rationale: agent language rule from AGENTS.md v13: seal record is team memory; translating user prose makes the corpus harder to read. Same rule applies on the read surface.

decisions.chose: first try requested lang → then EN → then [key]
decisions.rationale: silent empty is a visibility hazard; the bracketed key surfaces in the rendered page so the gap is impossible to miss

risks: The translation table is hand-edited Go; a typo or missed key surfaces as [key] in the page. Acceptable: visible failure is better than silent empty.

Agents who translate the seal text in spite of the language rule cause a mismatch between EN/ZH readers (same JSON either way, but uneven natural reading). The rule is in AGENTS.md / SKILL.md; eval will catch sustained violations.

anti_patterns.what: Translating intent content (title / what / why / decisions / risks / anti_patterns) at render time
anti_patterns.why: intent content is the team's long-term memory written in the user's language; render-side translation produces a fork between what the seal records and what readers see, breaking the agent language rule's whole point
those directories carry no UI text; duplicating bloats the site and forces dual-write on every JSON dump update

followups: Translate remaining page templates (open.html / files.html / review.html / risks.html / graph.html / actor.html / file.html / intent.html chrome) — current PR ships index.html + base.html chrome only
Surface a hover tooltip on the toggle showing the other language's name in the current language (e.g. EN page hover shows 'Chinese' in English)
If a third language ever lands, lift SupportedLanguages to a config so it isn't a code edit
